teamObjective.explanation: My hand is weak (non-trump diamonds 3-6) and my partner has only 2 cards, so the best team goal is to lead the lowest card and let my partner win the trick if possible.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner (player 2) is close to finishing with only 2 cards left and likely holds at least one card that can beat a low diamond 3, though I cannot know their exact cards.

opponentBelief.summary: Opponents each have 3 cards and no public plays have occurred, so they likely contain some higher non-trump cards or trumps that could beat my lead, but they may prefer to save them early.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Leading the diamond 3 is the cheapest possible lead. It minimizes the strength I commit, makes it easiest for my nearly-finished partner to win the trick, and preserves my slightly higher diamonds for later.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: Only marginally stronger than the 3 and still easily beaten; it makes it harder for my partner to win and wastes a slightly better card.
A middle card with no real winning power; keeping it is more useful for later tricks than spending it now.
My highest card but still weak; if it somehow wins, I remain stuck with three lower cards and have lost my best single.

risks: An opponent may beat the 3 with a higher card or trump and then lead aggressively.
My partner may not be able to beat even a 3 if their remaining cards are very low or a losing combination.
riskAssessment.mitigation: By leading the lowest card, I limit the strength opponents must spend to take control and keep my own hand as flexible as possible.
